era_context: Early Bronze Age in the Eastern Mediterranean and Near East. Egypt's Early Dynastic Period (c. 3100–2686 BCE) is in its early phase under the first unified pharaohs. Mesopotamia is transitioning toward or in the Early Dynastic III period. The Indus Valley Civilisation is developing. Writing is recent and not yet widespread.
disconfirming_evidence: No events identified for this year. The archaeological record for 3146 BCE is extremely sparse and undated to single-year precision. Chronologies for this period rely on radiocarbon dating with large margins of error and on king lists whose absolute dating is debated.
historiographic_note: This year falls in the same poorly documented period as 3145 BCE. No contemporary written records survive that can be confidently assigned to 3146 BCE. The historical record for this era is reconstructed from archaeological stratigraphy, radiocarbon dating, and later king lists, none of which permit year-by-year precision. The surviving record is heavily skewed toward elite and monumental contexts in the Near East and Egypt; vast regions of the world are archaeologically invisible or undocumented.
